A tornado tracked for two thirds of a mile near the intersection of Highway 61 and Highway 19 Blvd. It mostly went through fields just southwest and west of a school but did break or tip over about 12 to 15 trees. It was rated EF0 with maximum winds of 70 mph.

This tornado damaged several homes and farms. One home east-southeast of St Croix Falls had much of its roof removed. Hundreds of trees were broken or uprooted. It was rated EF1 with a path length of 5.0 miles. Maximum winds were estimated to be 90 mph and its maximum width was 100 yards. It dissipated at 920 pm. The tornado was preceded by downburst damage between Osceola and Dresser.

Late report after NWS assessed drone footage and other photographs from Morrison County emergency management. The tornado took roofs off of four barns or sheds, and dozens of trees were uprooted or broken, especially in Lastrup. Some trees or large branches landed on houses in Lastrup. The tornado dissipated just after moving through Lastrup. Rating is EF1. Path length was 7.5 miles long and the maximum winds were estimated at 90 mph.
